title: A Busy Sunday Afternoon
learningGoal: The learner will parse a narrative heavily saturated with the Present Continuous tense, actively identifying the 'Is/Am/Are + Verb + ING' structure and comprehending WH-question formats asking about ongoing actions.
instructions: Read the story about a busy park. Focus on what everyone is doing RIGHT NOW. Notice how every single action has two parts: The helper verb (is/are) and the action verb ending in '-ing'. Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: Pay attention to the questions in the story (e.g., 'What is he doing?'). See how the 'is' comes before 'he'?

readingText: It is Sunday afternoon. I am sitting in the big park in the city. Many people are here today. Everyone is doing something. What is the tall man doing? He is running. He is running very fast. What are the children doing? The children are playing. They are laughing and running. I see a woman under a tree. What is she doing? She is reading a red book. She is not talking. Over there, two boys are playing football. One boy is kicking the ball. The other boy is jumping. Look at the dog! The dog is running after a bird. Is the bird flying? Yes, the bird is flying away. The dog is barking. What am I doing? I am drinking cold water. I am watching the people. Wait, the sky is turning grey. What is happening? Oh no, it is raining. Now, the people are running. The woman is closing her book. The boys are stopping their game. Everyone is going home. I am walking to my car. The busy afternoon is ending.
textInTargetLanguage: It is Sunday afternoon. I am sitting in the big park in the city. Many people are here today. Everyone is doing something. What is the tall man doing? He is running. He is running very fast. What are the children doing? The children are playing. They are laughing and running. I see a woman under a tree. What is she doing? She is reading a red book. She is not talking. Over there, two boys are playing football. One boy is kicking the ball. The other boy is jumping. Look at the dog! The dog is running after a bird. Is the bird flying? Yes, the bird is flying away. The dog is barking. What am I doing? I am drinking cold water. I am watching the people. Wait, the sky is turning grey. What is happening? Oh no, it is raining. Now, the people are running. The woman is closing her book. The boys are stopping their game. Everyone is going home. I am walking to my car. The busy afternoon is ending.
baseLanguageSupportText: रविवार की दोपहर है। मैं शहर के बड़े पार्क में बैठा हूँ। आज यहाँ कई लोग हैं। हर कोई कुछ ना कुछ कर रहा है। लंबा आदमी क्या कर रहा है? वह दौड़ रहा है। वह बहुत तेज़ दौड़ रहा है। बच्चे क्या कर रहे हैं? बच्चे खेल रहे हैं। वे हंस रहे हैं और दौड़ रहे हैं। मैं एक पेड़ के नीचे एक महिला को देखता हूँ। वह क्या कर रही है? वह एक लाल किताब पढ़ रही है। वह बात नहीं कर रही है। वहाँ, दो लड़के फुटबॉल खेल रहे हैं। एक लड़का गेंद को लात मार रहा है। दूसरा लड़का कूद रहा है। कुत्ते को देखो! कुत्ता एक पक्षी के पीछे दौड़ रहा है। क्या पक्षी उड़ रहा है? हाँ, पक्षी उड़ रहा है। कुत्ता भौंक रहा है। मैं क्या कर रहा हूँ? मैं ठंडा पानी पी रहा हूँ। मैं लोगों को देख रहा हूँ। रुको, आसमान भूरा हो रहा है। क्या हो रहा है? ओह नहीं, बारिश हो रही है। अब, लोग दौड़ रहे हैं। महिला अपनी किताब बंद कर रही है। लड़के अपना खेल रोक रहे हैं। हर कोई घर जा रहा है। मैं अपनी कार की ओर चल रहा हूँ। व्यस्त दोपहर समाप्त हो रही है।

glossary.baseTranslation: कर रहा है / कर रही है
दौड़ रहा है / दौड़ रही है
पढ़ रहा है / पढ़ रही है

exampleSentence.baseTranslation: वह एक किताब पढ़ रही है।

glossary.baseTranslation: पी रहा है / पी रही है

sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: बच्चे क्या कर रहे हैं? (The 'Are' comes before 'the children')
मैं ठंडा पानी पी रहा हूँ। ('Am' is used with 'I')

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What is the tall man doing right now?
True or False: The text says 'The children playing'. (No 'are').
What is the woman under the tree doing?
What is the writer (the person telling the story) drinking?
Look at the sentence: 'It is raining.' What is the helper verb in this sentence?
At the end of the story, what does the woman do?
Where is the writer walking at the very end of the story?

expectedSummaryPoints: The learner recognizes the 'To be + verb-ing' formula for ongoing actions.
The learner intrinsically processes WH-Questions employing 'is/are' inversion.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You perfectly understood what everyone was doing in the park by tracking the 'ING' words.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember that happening-now actions are marked by 'ING'. Like reading, running, and playing.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Check your answer about the helper verb. In 'It is raining', 'raining' is the action. The helper verb is 'is'!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: It is Sunday afternoon. I am sitting in the big park in the city... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Any word ending in 'ing' means it is happening RIGHT NOW. Keep an eye out for it!
commonMistakes: Thinking 'The children playing' is a correct sentence in English. It always needs 'The children ARE playing'.
